summary: Public-source update center for the Garden Grove GKN Aerospace methyl methacrylate incident, with official emergency links, local news reports, health references, complaint routes, school-disruption information, and proof-preservation tools. Follow emergency officials first for active safety orders.

updates.title: City posts official video update and schedules May 26 special meeting
updates.summary: The City of Garden Grove emergency page posted a 2:20 p.m. official video update and said the regular May 26 City Council meeting has been canceled. The City said it will instead hold a special meeting on Tuesday, May 26 at 5:30 p.m. at the community meeting center focused on the incident, the City's response, and the latest available information, with translation services available.

updates.title: City page cites OCFA: evacuation zones being re-evaluated, no reentry yet
updates.summary: The City of Garden Grove emergency page posted an OCFA update from 2:03 p.m. PT saying that, because the BLEVE threat has been eliminated, fire and law-enforcement officials are re-evaluating evacuation zones. The same update warned residents not to enter evacuation zones yet and said officials planned a 6 p.m. press conference at the Incident Command Post in Los Alamitos.

updates.title: Governor says Presidential Emergency Declaration was approved for hazmat response
updates.summary: The Governor of California announced that President Trump approved California's request for a Presidential Emergency Declaration tied to the Garden Grove chemical incident. The Governor's office said the declaration allows FEMA to provide direct federal assistance with personnel, equipment, and specialized resources for life-saving response; it also said significant public health and environmental risks remain and evacuation orders continue to be in place.

updates.title: ABC7 reports OCFA says crisis is not fully over despite BLEVE threat reduction
updates.summary: ABC7 reported that OCFA Interim Fire Chief TJ McGovern clarified the crisis was not fully averted even though the most catastrophic BLEVE explosion threat had been eliminated after a tank crack relieved pressure. ABC7 quoted McGovern as saying a smaller explosion or leak remained possible and that residents should stay out of evacuation zones until officials deem reentry safe.

updates.title: KTLA reports OCFA says evacuations remain despite reduced explosion risk
updates.summary: KTLA reported that Orange County Fire Authority said evacuation orders remained in place even after officials determined the catastrophic BLEVE explosion risk had been eliminated. KTLA quoted OCFA as saying there was still an ongoing public-safety threat, that continuous atmospheric monitoring verified no chemical leak, and that officials planned a press conference later in the day; residents should keep following official evacuation-zone instructions.

updates.title: ABC7 says explosion threat eliminated while evacuations remain in place
updates.summary: ABC7 reported that Orange County Fire Authority officials said the GKN Aerospace tank has released its pressure, the tank's temperature stabilized and was reducing, and the catastrophic explosion threat had been eliminated. ABC7 also reported that evacuation orders remained in effect and that it was unclear when residents would be allowed to return home, so residents should keep following official City/OCFA instructions.

updates.title: ABC7 reports voting assistance planned for evacuees
updates.summary: ABC7 reported that Orange County election officials planned to send Registrar of Voters staff to emergency shelters to help displaced residents cast ballots, and that all voters, including evacuees, may visit any Orange County vote center. ABC7 also reported that the Garden Grove Sports and Recreation Center vote center was closed until further notice because the site was being used as an evacuation center.

updates.title: ABC7 reports crews still assessing whether explosion threat has reduced
updates.summary: ABC7 reported that firefighters continued a high-risk assessment at the GKN Aerospace tank after an overnight operation to determine whether a crack has relieved enough pressure to reduce the explosion threat. ABC7 cited OCFA as saying there was one known crack, no active chemical leak, and crews were still assessing the tank and surrounding conditions; residents should continue following official evacuation-zone instructions.

resources.description: Shelter-center information, animal-shelter notes, local hotlines, Ready.CA.gov alerts, and aging/disability resources.

resources.description: Official complaint route for odor, emissions, air-quality, or facility-related concerns in Southern California.

resources.description: Reporting on the potential blast-zone map, evacuation geography, and public-safety response.

resources.title: CBS Los Angeles DA investigation report
resources.description: Reporting that the Orange County District Attorney opened an investigation and sought whistleblower information.

resources.title: Proof checklist on this page
resources.description: Receipts, photos, alerts, symptom logs, wage records, business closure notes, cleanup invoices, and property records to preserve.

resources.title: Free Berhe Jones screening
resources.description: Send a short conflict-safe summary for attorney-supervised review. Keep sensitive records for secure follow-up.
